PreToolUse.matcher: tool == "Bash" && tool_input.command matches "(npm run dev|pnpm( run)? dev|yarn dev|bun run dev)"

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
input=$(cat)
cmd=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.tool_input.command // ""')

# Block dev servers that aren't run in tmux
echo '[Hook] BLOCKED: Dev server must run in tmux for log access' >&2
echo '[Hook] Use this command instead:' >&2
echo "[Hook] tmux new-session -d -s dev 'npm run dev'" >&2
echo '[Hook] Then: tmux attach -t dev' >&2
exit 1

PreToolUse.description: Block dev servers outside tmux - ensures you can access logs

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
input=$(cat)
if [ -z "$TMUX" ]; then
  echo '[Hook] Consider running in tmux for session persistence' >&2
  echo '[Hook] tmux new -s dev  |  tmux attach -t dev' >&2
fi
echo "$input"

PreToolUse.description: Reminder to use tmux for long-running commands

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
# Open editor for review before pushing
echo '[Hook] Review changes before push...' >&2
# Uncomment your preferred editor:
# zed . 2>/dev/null
# code . 2>/dev/null
# cursor . 2>/dev/null
echo '[Hook] Press Enter to continue with push or Ctrl+C to abort...' >&2
read -r

PreToolUse.description: Pause before git push to review changes

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
# Block creation of unnecessary documentation files
input=$(cat)
file_path=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // ""')

if [[ "$file_path" =~ \.(md|txt)$ ]] && [[ ! "$file_path" =~ (README|CLAUDE|AGENTS|CONTRIBUTING)\.md$ ]]; then
  echo "[Hook] BLOCKED: Unnecessary documentation file creation" >&2
  echo "[Hook] File: $file_path" >&2
  echo "[Hook] Use README.md for documentation instead" >&2
  exit 1
fi

echo "$input"

PreToolUse.description: Block creation of random .md files - keeps docs consolidated

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
# Auto-detect PR creation and log useful info
input=$(cat)
cmd=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.tool_input.command')

if echo "$cmd" | grep -qE 'gh pr create'; then
  output=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.tool_output.output // ""')
  pr_url=$(echo "$output" | grep -oE 'https://github.com/[^/]+/[^/]+/pull/[0-9]+')
  
  if [ -n "$pr_url" ]; then
    echo "[Hook] PR created: $pr_url" >&2
    echo "[Hook] Checking GitHub Actions status..." >&2
    repo=$(echo "$pr_url" | sed -E 's|https://github.com/([^/]+/[^/]+)/pull/[0-9]+|\1|')
    pr_num=$(echo "$pr_url" | sed -E 's|.*/pull/([0-9]+)|\1|')
    echo "[Hook] To review PR: gh pr review $pr_num --repo $repo" >&2
  fi
fi

echo "$input"

PostToolUse.description: Log PR URL and provide review command after PR creation

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
# Auto-lint-fix: format + lint + auto-fix loop (Aider pattern)
input=$(cat)
file_path=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // ""')

if [ -z "$file_path" ] || [ ! -f "$file_path" ]; then
  echo "$input"
  exit 0
fi

# Step 1: Prettier format
if command -v prettier >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  prettier --write "$file_path" 2>/dev/null
fi

# Step 2: ESLint auto-fix (if available)
dir=$(dirname "$file_path")
project_root="$dir"
while [ "$project_root" != "/" ] && [ ! -f "$project_root/package.json" ]; do
  project_root=$(dirname "$project_root")
done

if [ -f "$project_root/.eslintrc.json" ] || [ -f "$project_root/.eslintrc.js" ] || [ -f "$project_root/.eslintrc.cjs" ] || [ -f "$project_root/eslint.config.js" ] || [ -f "$project_root/eslint.config.mjs" ]; then
  cd "$project_root"
  npx eslint --fix "$file_path" 2>/dev/null
  
  # Step 3: Check if lint errors remain after auto-fix
  lint_errors=$(npx eslint "$file_path" --format compact 2>/dev/null | grep -c 'Error' || true)
  if [ "$lint_errors" -gt 0 ]; then
    echo "[Hook] LINT: $lint_errors error(s) remain after auto-fix in $file_path" >&2
    npx eslint "$file_path" --format compact 2>/dev/null | head -5 >&2
  fi
fi

echo "$input"

PostToolUse.description: Auto-lint-fix: Prettier format → ESLint --fix → report remaining errors

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
# TypeScript check with actionable error output
input=$(cat)
file_path=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // ""')

if [ -n "$file_path" ] && [ -f "$file_path" ]; then
  dir=$(dirname "$file_path")
  project_root="$dir"
  while [ "$project_root" != "/" ] && [ ! -f "$project_root/package.json" ]; do
    project_root=$(dirname "$project_root")
  done
  
  if [ -f "$project_root/tsconfig.json" ]; then
    cd "$project_root"
    tsc_output=$(npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false 2>&1 | grep "$(basename "$file_path")" | head -10)
    if [ -n "$tsc_output" ]; then
      error_count=$(echo "$tsc_output" | wc -l)
      echo "[Hook] TSC: $error_count type error(s) in $file_path" >&2
      echo "$tsc_output" >&2
      echo "[Hook] Fix these type errors before proceeding" >&2
    fi
  fi
fi

echo "$input"

PostToolUse.description: TypeScript check with error count and actionable output

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
# Warn about console.log in edited files
input=$(cat)
file_path=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // ""')

if [ -n "$file_path" ] && [ -f "$file_path" ]; then
  console_logs=$(grep -n "console\\.log" "$file_path" 2>/dev/null || true)
  
  if [ -n "$console_logs" ]; then
    echo "[Hook] WARNING: console.log found in $file_path" >&2
    echo "$console_logs" | head -5 >&2
    echo "[Hook] Remove console.log before committing" >&2
  fi
fi

echo "$input"

PostToolUse.description: Warn about console.log statements after edits

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
# Re-inject critical project context after context compaction
input=$(cat)

echo '[Hook] Context compaction detected - reminding to reload critical files' >&2
echo '[Hook] Re-read these files to restore project context:' >&2

# Check for CLAUDE.md
if [ -f 'CLAUDE.md' ]; then
  echo '[Hook]   - CLAUDE.md (project instructions)' >&2
fi

# Check for .claude/settings.json
if [ -f '.claude/settings.json' ]; then
  echo '[Hook]   - .claude/settings.json (project settings)' >&2
fi

# Check for active todo/task state
if [ -f '.auto/state/loop-state.json' ]; then
  echo '[Hook]   - .auto/state/loop-state.json (active task state)' >&2
fi

echo '[Hook] Use Read tool to reload these files before continuing work' >&2

echo "$input"

PostCompaction.description: Re-inject critical project context after context window compaction

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
# Warn if user prompt contains potential secrets
input=$(cat)
prompt=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.prompt // ""')

# Check for common secret patterns
if echo "$prompt" | grep -qE '(sk-[a-zA-Z0-9]{20,}|ghp_[a-zA-Z0-9]{36}|gho_[a-zA-Z0-9]{36}|glpat-[a-zA-Z0-9\-]{20,}|xoxb-[0-9]{10,}|AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16})'; then
  echo '[Hook] WARNING: Potential secret/API key detected in your prompt!' >&2
  echo '[Hook] Patterns found: sk-*, ghp_*, gho_*, glpat-*, xoxb-*, AKIA*' >&2
  echo '[Hook] Consider using environment variables instead of pasting keys directly' >&2
fi

# Pass through input data without blocking
echo "$input"

UserPromptSubmit.description: Warn if user prompt contains potential secrets (API keys, tokens)

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
# Remind team lead to check task list when a teammate goes idle
input=$(cat)
agent_name=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.agent_name // "unknown"')

echo "[Hook] Teammate '$agent_name' is now idle" >&2
echo '[Hook] Consider checking TaskList for unassigned tasks to keep the team productive' >&2
echo '[Hook] Use SendMessage to assign new work or send shutdown_request if done' >&2

echo "$input"

TeammateIdle.description: Remind team lead to check task list and assign work when teammate goes idle

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
# Quality gate: check for uncommitted changes before allowing task completion
input=$(cat)

if git rev-parse --git-dir > /dev/null 2>&1; then
  uncommitted=$(git status --porcelain 2>/dev/null | head -20)
  
  if [ -n "$uncommitted" ]; then
    echo '[Hook] QUALITY GATE: Uncommitted changes detected!' >&2
    echo '[Hook] Files with pending changes:' >&2
    echo "$uncommitted" | head -10 >&2
    echo '[Hook] Commit or stash changes before marking task as completed' >&2
    exit 2
  fi
fi

echo "$input"

TaskCompleted.description: Quality gate - block task completion if uncommitted changes exist (exit 2 = reject)

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
# Final check for console.logs in modified files
input=$(cat)

if git rev-parse --git-dir > /dev/null 2>&1; then
  modified_files=$(git diff --name-only HEAD 2>/dev/null | grep -E '\.(ts|tsx|js|jsx)$' || true)
  
  if [ -n "$modified_files" ]; then
    has_console=false
    while IFS= read -r file; do
      if [ -f "$file" ]; then
        if grep -q "console\.log" "$file" 2>/dev/null; then
          echo "[Hook] WARNING: console.log found in $file" >&2
          has_console=true
        fi
      fi
    done <<< "$modified_files"
    
    if [ "$has_console" = true ]; then
      echo "[Hook] Remove console.log statements before committing" >&2
    fi
  fi
fi

echo "$input"

Stop.description: Final audit for console.log in modified files before session ends
